title: The Exile of the Kufans Whom ('Uthman] Sent [to Syria
description: Syria J193 The authorities (ahl al-siyar) disagree on this matter. Sayf' s account was transmitted to me in writing by al-SariShu'ayb-(Sayf)-Muhammad and Talhah: Said b. al-'A^ used to receive visits only from those long settled in Kufah, the leading men among the veterans of the earliest battles in Iraq (wujuh ahl al-ayyam) and of al-Qadisiyyah, the Qur'an reciters (qurra')194 [2908] among the Ba^rans, and the pious-minded. These were his confidants when he retired [to his private quarters ].

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 15
